data.title: Memory read-path: inject confirmed memories into model context (provenance-gated)

data.body: From the same conversation. Today memory is WRITE-ONLY: memory.py consider() gates what gets stored (untrusted->quarantine, sensitive->reject, trusted->memories with may_influence_actions=False), but loop.py/prompts.py never read memories back -- stored memories never reach the model. This task wires the READ path: inject stored memories into model context (likely a system-prompt section assembled in handle_request/run_loop). Provenance rules to respect: only memories may be injected; quarantined candidates NEVER; respect may_influence_actions=False (memories may inform ANSWERS but must not drive tool/action decisions until human-confirmed -- decide in planning how to enforce that distinction, e.g. labeled 'background context, do not act on' framing). Open design points for planning: how memories get human-confirmed (no UI/CLI exists -- may need a small confirm command or API), selection/limit strategy (all vs recent N vs keyword match), and trace event for injected-memory hashes (never raw content).
